marketState: neutral, mildly bearish skew short-term with a genuinely bullish weather wildcard
summary: Front-month NG trades at $3.215, about $0.29 above the current fair-value estimate of $2.93 (band $2.43-$3.43), so price sits inside the band but on the rich side rather than cheap. Weather demand is the standout bullish input: 1-7D demand is +10.7% vs the 5-year norm (rangePos 0.78) and 8-16D demand is +26.1% vs norm and near the top of its 5-year range (rangePos 0.96), pointing to strong summer cooling-driven power burn, though the 24h change (-11.9) shows that heat is already cooling off its recent peak. Working against that: this week's storage build beat forecast by 13 Bcf (bearish surprise) and the surplus vs the 5-year average widened to +175 Bcf, managed money remains net short (COT percentile 31 of 5 years), and technicals lean bearish on the daily/weekly timeframes (bearish trend, negative MACD, 50<200 EMA) even though hourly/4h readings are neutral-to-mild-bullish. Seasonality data (price vs typical-year median, probability-higher stats, reliability) is entirely null in this snapshot, which is the single highest-priority input for the medium-term view, so the 3-12 week case cannot be built with confidence right now. Net read: short-term favors fading the premium to fair value given soft technicals and the bearish storage print, while the longer-term stance stays on the sidelines until seasonality data returns and confirms whether the current storage surplus is being eroded by the hot 8-16D outlook.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality (price vs typical-year median, probability-higher-in-N-weeks, reliability) is completely missing from the snapshot, and it is the top-priority input for a 3-12 week call. The remaining fundamentals are mixed and roughly offsetting: a +175 Bcf storage surplus and a bearish weekly surprise argue for downside, while near-record 8-16D cooling demand (rangePos 0.96 vs 5y) argues the surplus could erode quickly if the heat persists, and COT positioning (percentile 31) is short but not at an extreme that signals capitulation either way.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for seasonality/vsMedian data to populate and confirm the seasonal price bias, or for a second storage report to show the surplus narrowing (i.e., demand catching up with the hot 8-16D forecast) before taking a directional medium-term position.

shortTerm.rationale: Front is trading $0.29 (about 10%) above today's fair-value estimate versus a mostly FAIR next-month contract, daily/weekly technicals are bearish with negative MACD and a 50<200 EMA cross, and this week's storage build beat forecast (bearish surprise). The futures curve also shows slight backwardation into Sep/Oct (front priced above the next two contracts), consistent with the current heat premium fading as the 8-16D demand pulse (change24h -11.9) has already started rolling over.
shortTerm.invalidation: A sustained close above $3.44 (top of the now fair-value band) or a fresh acceleration in the 8-16D heat/demand reading would invalidate the short and argue for standing aside or flipping long toward $3.60-3.80.

Front price $3.215 trading ~10% above today's fair value of $2.93
Storage surplus widened to +175 Bcf vs 5y on a bearish +13 Bcf surprise build
Managed money net short (-64,808 contracts, 31st percentile of 5 years)

risks: Seasonality data is entirely missing, so the medium-term seasonal edge cannot be verified this cycle
8-16D heat is near a 5-year extreme (rangePos 0.96) - a further intensification could spark a short squeeze against the net-short COT position and invalidate the short-term short
